claims.raw_sentence: Sales up 15% YoverY and grew across all geographic segments.
Among tablets priced over $200, iPad's share was 89%
Among those planning to buy tablets, purchase intent for iPad was over 70%
$246b of this cash, 94% of total, was outside US.
$246b of this cash, 94% of total, was outside US.
Issued equivalent of $10.8b in new euro and US dollar denominated debt, including second green bond.
Spent $4.5b on repurchases of 30.4m Co. shares through open market transactions.
Launched new $3b ASR program, resulting in initial delivery and retirement of 15.6m shares.
Now completed $222.9b of $300b capital return program, including $158.5b in share repurchases.
in constant-currency terms, we were actually up 6% in Mainland China
There's -- we've created 2 million jobs in the U.S. and we're incredibly proud of that.
about $50 billion worth of goods and services
typically, in the fiscal Q4, Apple builds considerable iPhone channel inventory like 2 million or 3 million units.
iPhone grew about 2% year-over-year this quarter
App Store customers broke all-time records, including $3b in purchases in Dec. alone making it the App Store's single best month ever.
Co.'s developer community has now earned over $60b, including over $20b in 2016 alone.
On 01/31/17, Board of Directors has declared cash dividend of $0.57 per share of common stock

Revenue reached an all-time quarterly record of $8.5b in Sept. qtr.
Grew more than 30% in Mexico, Middle East, Turkey and Central and Eastern Europe.
Aforementioned results helped to fuel overall growth of over 20% from emerging markets.
Diluted EPS $2.07; new Sept. qtr. record.

For corporate smartphone buyers, iOS customer satisfaction was 95% and of those planning to purchase smartphones in Dec. qtr., 80% plan to purchase iPhone; highest score for iPhone in history of survey.
Set all-time quarterly record of $8.5b, up 34% YoverY.
On run-rate basis, excluding adjustments, services growth of 24% was terrific and highest that Co. has experienced this year.
cash dividend of $0.63 per share of common stock
sales were up 7% in the September quarter
